ops.title: Transcript persistence depends on pool accounts trusting the worktree parent
ops.body: Interactive claude in an UNTRUSTED cwd runs and answers turns but persists no transcript jsonl — and cc-orchestrate's tailer derives all agent status from transcripts, so an untrusted worktree means tokens:0/no derived status forever. Today (2026-07-16) this cannot bite: worktrees live under ~/.cc-orchestrate/worktrees, /Users/yasyf is trusted (hasTrustDialogAccepted) in all 17 cc-pool account overlays, and a trusted parent suppresses the child dialog and enables persistence. Latent fragility: a freshly-added pool account that never trusted home would starve the tailer for any agent ccp places on it. Deliberately NOT hardened (2026-07-16, house no-defensive-coding rule; evidence suggests ccp seeds new accounts' trust from a common base). If agents ever show tokens:0 with a healthy pane again: check the selected account's .claude.json trust for the worktree path FIRST, then ccp daemon health (a stale ccp daemon + active 429 pool backoff was live during the smoke that first showed this symptom). Note: `claude -p` print mode always persists transcripts regardless of trust — it cannot be used to test this. Related note: ccp launch is unconditional-when-installed.
